observations.summary: The observer hidden in the olive tree shadows reflects a timeless watcher who has seen the same scene unfold over a thousand years, paralleling the motif of enduring cycles and the slow, inevitable grinding of time to dust.

source_evidence.quote: The observer, hidden in the lengthening shadows of the olive tree, noted all of this with the patient interest of one who has watched this same scene play out in a thousand variations across a thousand years.

observations.target_hint: The motif of enduring cycles and slow passage of time is echoed throughout the narrative, including references to the great wheel turning and grinding all things to dust.

target_evidence.quote: And the great wheel would continue its slow, inexorable turning, grinding all things eventually to dust—though some dust, the observer thought, shone brighter than others.
